text:
Team GB boxer Rosie Eccles was left in tears after her hugely controversial defeat at theParisOlympics, a decision that was booed by fans in attendance.

Eccles, who won gold forWalesat theCommonwealth Gamesin 2022, thought she had done enough in her opening round match against Poland’s Aneta Rygielska in the 66kg category.

The Briton appeared to be firmly in control of the fight with her opponent also deducted a point in the final round.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for latest news on the Paris Olympics

But ringside judges awarded the victory to Rygielska, stunning Eccles and drawing boos from the crowd.

‘I knew I’d won the fight. I’m really, really shocked,’ a tearful Eccles said after the bout.

‘In my mind I won that fight. I don’t know what to say other than I am really grateful for everyone that helped me get here. I appreciate you all and I’m sorry I couldn’t get it over the line.

‘I knew she was a tricky opponent, but I felt I did the job. I stayed disciplined and kept to my game and I thought I had done enough.

‘I’ve devoted my whole life for an Olympic medal but unfortunately for me, and Charley as well yesterday, it wasn’t to be.’

BBC boxing pundit Steve Bunce was among those stunned by the decision. ‘I’m genuinely shocked, I really did think she’d [Rosie Eccles] get it. That was a brutal outcome’ Bunce said.

‘We’ve got independent judges here. They went with the one who got deducted a point and she actually fouled. The two judges who had it even voted for the woman who had an infringement. I’m really annoyed. It’s a bad decision.’

Eccles became the second British fighter to suffer an early elimination after Charley Davison fell to defeat against Hatice Akbas.

The 30-year-old, appearing in her second Olympic Games was also on the wrong end of a tight decision.

The Olympics is no stranger to controversial boxing decisions. In 2016, Irish fighter Michael Conlan blasted judges after his defeat to Vladimir Nikitin.

Conlan labelled boxing authorities ‘corrupt’ and vowed to never fight in the Olympics again in a wild post-fight interview.

title: Team GB boxer left in tears as fans boo controversial defeat at Olympics | Metro News
description: Team GB boxer Rosie Eccles suffered a controversial defeat at the Paris Olympics to Aneta Rygielska of Poland.
